data.title: Premium Brands gains as activist investor sees potential 75% upside
data.description:
Premium Brands (PBH:CA) rose 1.5% as an activist investor suggested the shares could see 75% upside from current levels.

Alta Fox, which has a 1.5% stake in Premium Brands (PBH:CA), argues that the company's shares are "materially undervalued."

"The stock now trades at decade-low valuation multiples more reflective of ex-growth, structurally challenged peers despite Premium Brands’ consistent organic volume growth, which has accelerated to double digits in the last two quarters," Alta Fox said in a statement  on Wednesday.

Alta Fox wants Premium Brands to be clearer with messaging around its 2027 earnings power. Specifically, the company should provide an updated FY2027 EBITDA target and commit to a defined FY2027 free cash flow target. 

Premium Brands (PBH:CA) didn't immediately return Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Premium Brands (PBH:CA) is set to report Q1 results next Thursday.

data.title: Element Solutions projects $665M-$685M 2026 adjusted EBITDA while lifting CapEx to $75M-$100M
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Element Solutions (ESI) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "Element Solutions started 2026 strong. We reported a record quarter yesterday that demonstrates ongoing success with our strategy of penetrating the highest value, fastest-growing subsegments in our addressable markets." (CEO & Director Benjamin Gliklich)
  • "Sales in our Electronics segment grew 15% organically as activity accelerated across our supply chain in support of the ongoing AI infrastructure build-out." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "Forecasts from customers are increasing and innovation cycles are accelerating." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "Organic net sales grew 10%, and constant currency adjusted EBITDA increased 21% year-over-year." (President of Enterprise Operations & CFO Carey Dorman)
  • "Pass-through metals revenue was $256 million in the first quarter of 2026 and $101 million in the fourth quarter of 2025." (CFO Dorman)

Outlook

  • "We're raising our adjusted EBITDA guidance to a range of $665 million to $685 million for the full year." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "We expect second quarter adjusted EBITDA in the range of $155 million to $170 million, with demand conditions sequentially similar to the first quarter and taking into consideration some risk from raw material and logistics inflation that we may not recapture immediately despite ongoing sourcing and pricing actions." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "We now expect 2026 adjusted EPS growth in the high teens on a full year basis." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • Compared with the prior quarter, management moved from "Our 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance range is $650 million to $670 million" to "$665 million to $685 million" and added that the 2026 range "contemplates a less favorable FX tailwind than we expected a few months ago." (CEO & Director Gliklich)

Financial Results

  • "On this new basis, adjusted EBITDA margin improved 170 bps year-over-year to 27.8% this quarter." (CFO Dorman)
  • "Adjusted EPS grew 21% in the first quarter, largely reflecting the underlying demand improvement in our Electronics business and offset by higher interest costs associated with our recent acquisition activities." (CFO Dorman)
  • "Micromax, which we own for 2 months of this quarter, is not included in our organic net sales growth calculation, but contributed roughly $65 million to reported sales in the quarter." (CFO Dorman)
  • "Free cash flow was negative." (CFO Dorman)
  • "CapEx in the quarter was $25 million" and "we now expect to invest between $75 million and $100 million in CapEx this year." (CFO Dorman)

Q&A

  • Bhavesh Lodaya, BMO Capital Markets Equity Research: Within Electronics, how much of the 15% organic growth was volume vs. pricing/mix, and how to think about continuation? CEO Gliklich: "most of our growth in the first quarter has been volume driven" and "our outlook is for a continued robust volume environment."
  • Bhavesh Lodaya, BMO Capital Markets Equity Research: Where is stepped-up CapEx going, and what about plant consolidation? CEO Gliklich: "Kuprion is something that we're investing heavily in" and "in our Industrial business, we've been reducing our footprint" including "a large site consolidation project in one site in Europe."
  • Joshua Spector, UBS Investment Bank, Research Division: What is driving higher confidence in the guide, and is there better demand visibility? CEO Gliklich: "we've got confidence in the durability of this acceleration in demand through 2026 from data center and associated investment" and noted Micromax "seasonality and lumpiness" as a factor in phasing.
  • Joshua Spector, UBS Investment Bank, Research Division: Is Micromax contributing more than initially expected? CEO Gliklich: "it's a stronger result from an organic perspective" and "we are expecting better contribution from Micromax on a full year basis" while "still working" on seasonality.
  • Christopher Parkinson, Wolfe Research, LLC: Any changed expectations across Electronics and what’s improved? CEO Gliklich: "we do believe that PCB growth will outstrip what was originally forecast" and "the smartphone market... is better for us than forecast."
  • Christopher Parkinson, Wolfe Research, LLC: Kuprion commercialization trajectory? CEO Gliklich: "Commercialization is good" and "the note of caution I'd strike is just supply chain and our ability to meet demand."
  • Michael Harrison, Seaport Research Partners: Is metal volatility disrupting demand; threat or opportunity; and any changes to hedging/pass-through approach? CEO Gliklich: "higher metal prices have not had an impact on demand as yet" and cited competitors "running into cash flow issues" as a benefit; on hedging, "we're comfortable with the practice and don't expect anything to change."
  • John Ezekiel Roberts, Mizuho Securities USA LLC, Research Division: What was industry growth in Electronics and any updated thoughts on memory impacts? CEO Gliklich: "The data isn't out yet" and "our organic results are outstripping the overall industry"; on memory, "it accelerates the transition of our business away from consumer electronics and towards enterprise applications."
  • Rock Hoffman Blasko, BofA Securities, Research Division: Is weaker smartphone shipment outlook in guidance, and timing/scale of pricing actions? CEO Gliklich: "our business skews towards the high end" and "we believe we're insulated"; on inflation recovery, "Historically... we've put in place surcharges" and "it's tens of millions of dollars of risk, but we expect to be able to mitigate most of it over the course of the year."
  • Peter Osterland, Truist Securities, Inc., Research Division: Any demand destruction in Industrial and sustainability of Offshore growth? CEO Gliklich: "our expectation is for weaker demand growth in the Industrial Solutions business"; on Offshore, "it should be a good year... another year of high single-digit organic top line."
  • Jonathan Tanwanteng, CJS Securities, Inc.: Any electronic supply chain constraints in outlook, and EV vs. total auto expectations? CEO Gliklich: "There is risk there" but "we skew disproportionately towards the higher end"; on EV, "Our EV-exposed business has been outgrowing even EV units because we're taking share with power electronics."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts: slightly positive, with repeated validation of outperformance and durability (e.g., "Congrats on a strong Q1" and "Really nice Q1") alongside targeted risk-testing on metals, inflation, smartphones, and industrial demand.
  • Management: slightly positive in prepared remarks ("We had a great start to the year") but more cautious on macro risks in discussion ("geopolitical events have created a more complex macro environment" and "expect increased variance in quarterly earnings driven by swings in metal prices").
  • Versus last quarter, management’s language increased macro caution (Q4: "we expect market conditions to largely resemble late 2025"; Q1: "more complex macro environment") while confidence in Electronics demand remained consistent (Q4: "demand remains robust"; Q1: "Underlying demand in the high-end electronics market remains strong").

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The full-year adjusted EBITDA guide moved from "$650 million to $670 million" to "$665 million to $685 million," and Q1 added explicit commentary on "softer demand in Industrial Solutions" and a "less favorable FX tailwind." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • Capital intensity expectations increased as the company shifted from "For 2026, we expect capital expenditures of approximately $75 million" to "we now expect to invest between $75 million and $100 million in CapEx this year" tied to customer-led growth capacity and consolidation projects. (CFO Dorman)
  • Portfolio and integration emphasis stayed active across both calls, with Q1 updating that Micromax and EFC "are off to a solid start" and "Integration is on track," versus Q4 framing them as newly closed acquisitions expected to "flourish and grow faster and more efficiently." (CEO & Director Gliklich)

Risks and Concerns

  • "We're seeing signs of inflationary pressure" and "further supply chain disruptions and the impacts to global demand resulting from higher energy prices creates risk for our suppliers, our customers and ultimately for us." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "We expect increased variance in quarterly earnings driven by swings in metal prices." (CEO & Director Gliklich)
  • "Global industrial weakness continued this quarter" and management said it remains "cautious about our European industrial demand outlook." (CFO Dorman)

Final Takeaway

Management described Q1 as a "record quarter" driven by Electronics demand tied to the "AI infrastructure build-out," alongside double-digit organic growth in Electronics and raised full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance to $665 million-$685 million. At the same time, management highlighted a more uncertain operating backdrop—citing inflation, metal-driven earnings variability, and softer Industrial demand—while increasing planned 2026 CapEx to $75 million-$100 million to support customer-led capacity additions (including Kuprion) and select consolidation projects, with a stated intent to stay "on the offensive" in high-value electronics niches.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Summers Value Fund adds TBPH, exits ADMA among Q1 moves
data.description:
The Summers Value Fund LP returned -2.7% net in Q1 2026, underperforming the Russell 2000 Index ETF, which returned 0.9%, and the Russell 2000 Value Index ETF, which returned 4.9%.

According to its quarterly fund letter, the largest contributors to the fund's performance in the first quarter included Liquidia (LQDA), Avanos Medical (AVNS), and Consensus Cloud Solutions (CCSI).

The largest detractors were Electromed (ELMD) and Embecta (EMBC).

During the quarter, the Fund initiated a new position in Theravance (TBPH) and exited ADMA Biologics (ADMA), as it fell short of the Fund’s expectations, and the Fund identified more attractive opportunities for its capital.

The Fund also strengthened its position in Embecta (EMBC) following a sell-off after the company narrowed its fiscal 2026 guidance to the low end of the range.

Source: Q1 fund letter

data.title: Generac surges after raising full-year sales forecast on growing data center backlog
data.description:
Generac (GNRC) up 14.5% in Wednesday's trading to $255.87, its highest intraday level in more than three and a half years, after easily beating Q1 adjusted earnings expectations, lifted by ​solid demand for backup generators and a growing ‌backlog from data center customers, and raising full-year guidance.

Q1 profit rose to $73M, or $1.24/share, from $44M, or $0.73/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and revenue rose 12.5% Y/Y to $1.06B, as sales ​in the residential segment edged up 1% Y/Y to $549M and sales in the ​commercial and industrial segment jumped 28% to $510M.

Generac (GNRC) said it now sees FY 2026 ⁠revenue growth in the mid-to-high teens percent range, which translates to a range of $4.84B-$5B, compared to the $4.85B FactSet consensus estimate and up from the company's prior forecast of the mid-teens range, and boosts guidance for adjusted EBITDA margin to 18.5%-19.5% from its prior outlook of 18%-19%.

Generac (GNRC) "reported 1Q revenue slightly above expectations, primarily driven by the C&I business," according to J.P. Morgan analyst Mark Strouse, who rates the stock at Overweight. "Gross margin was particularly strong, driven by price-cost realization, and resulting in a solid EPS beat."

"We are continuing to build momentum in the large and rapidly growing data center end market as we are in the final stages of vendor approval with multiple hyperscale customers and have expanded our backlog for these products with both new and existing customers," President and CEO Aaron Jagdfeld said.

data.title: Pfizer posts late-stage trial win for multiple myeloma therapy Elrexfio
data.description:
Pfizer (PFE) announced on Wednesday that Elrexfio, a treatment for a type of blood cancer called multiple myeloma, reached the primary endpoint in a late-stage trial, raising prospects for the immunotherapy, which is already available in the U.S. under the FDA’s accelerated pathway.

Citing topline data, the company stated that its Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 study met the main goal, as patients who received Elrexfio survived longer without cancer (progression‑free survival) with a statistically significant and clinically meaningful effect.

The injectable, which was granted the FDA's accelerated approval in 2023 as a late-line option for RRMM, indicated a safety and tolerability profile in line with its established safety profile, Pfizer (PFE) added.

The open-label study was designed to evaluate Elrexfio as a single agent versus standard of care in nearly 500 adults with relapsed or refractory MM who had already received one or more prior lines of therapy.

While the trial is set to continue to assess overall survival, a key secondary endpoint, Pfizer (PFE) plans to share the existing results with global regulators.

data.title: Canada approves generic version of Novo's Ozempic
data.description:
• Health Canada on Tuesday approved a generic version of Novo Nordisk's blockbuster GLP-1 semaglutide injection, which is branded as Ozempic for type 2 diabetes.
  • The approval was granted to India-based Dr. Reddy's Laboratories. The authorization does not apply to semaglutide's use for weight loss, which is marketed as Wegovy.
  • Health Canada noted that eight other applications for generic semaglutide are pending review. They include Teva Canada (TEVA), Sandoz Canada (SDZNY)(SDZXF), Apotex, and Taro Pharmaceuticals. 
  • Canada is now the first G7 country to approve a semaglutide generic.

data.title: Robinhood stock tumbles after weak Q1 results, mixed April trends
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD) stock slumped 14% in Wednesday morning trading after Q1 earnings disappointed investors, and its trends in April failed to buoy confidence.

Barclays analyst Benjamin Budish said the Q1 miss was driven by take rates on options and crypto and light net interest income due to a "variety of factors." Furthermore, April trends were mixed with strength in equities and options volumes but continued fee compression.

He attributes the weaker-than-expected net interest income, in part, to "complexity in modeling margin balance funding costs."

On the positive side, equities and options activity are improving, prediction markets fared better than feared, and April's net deposits suggest annualized net deposit growth of at least 20%, Budish said. "Equities and options volumes are on track for their second highest month in April, implying volumes could be up at least 6% m/m for equities and 10% for options, ahead of broader market volumes (industry options volumes trending +1% m/m, notional equities trending 17% lower)," he wrote in a note to clients.

Barclays, which has an Overweight rating on the stock, reduced its price target for Robinhood (HOOD) to $82 from $89. The firm cut its estimates for HOOD's  Q2 adjusted EPS to $0.42, down 20% from its prior estimate, and FY2026 adjusted EPS to $1.91, down 13% from the firm's previous estimate.

Morgan Stanley analyst Michael J. Cyprys notes that Robinhood's (HOOD) month-to-date deposit growth implies 19.6% annualized growth, slowing from Q1's 22% growth rate. Meanwhile, "crypto volume appears broadly similar to a weak 1Q."

The analyst noted the encouraging equity and options volume but acknowledged the mixed nature of the month-to-date trends. "April volume commentary was constructive, but take-rate dynamics remain a key near-term debate after softer 1Q transaction revenues," Cyprys wrote in a note to clients.

Overall, Morgan Stanley, with an Equalweight rating on HOOD, remains constructive on the company's "long-term growth opportunity as management continues to ship new products, broaden the ecosystem beyond trading, and deepen wallet share with Gold customers."

Wolfe Research analyst Steven Chubak lowered his estimates for 2027 GAAP EPS by 11% to $2.40, citing a combination of lower take rates, higher expenses, and lower net interest income. However, he remains constructive on the stock, maintaining its Outperform rating.

"Despite near-term headwinds to activity, the long-term investment case for HOOD remains intact, as share gains in active trader, an expanded product offering, wider geographic footprint, and high incremental margins/A.I. efficiencies should translate into a sustainable 20%+ EPS CAGR," Chubak wrote.

The SA Quant system rates Robinhood (HOOD) a Sell, while the average SA Analyst rating and average Wall Street rating are Buy.

data.title: RIOT Platform Q1 preview: Make-or-break earnings as analysts cut bets
data.description:
Riot Platforms (RIOT) will report its Q1 2026 earnings on April 30 at 4:30 PM EST, and the stock is already under pressure. In the last week, it has fallen ~15% from ~$18.66 to ~$15.98 as investors stay cautious before results.

Street expectations are weak. The company is expected to post a consensus estimate loss of -$0.83 EPS with revenue of ~$130.58M.

The estimate revision trend is clearly negative—0 upward vs. 8 downward EPS revisions and 1 upward vs. 12 downward revenue revisions in the past 3 months.

On the other hand, as per its unaudited production and operations Q1 updates, the company sold ~3.78K Bitcoin (BTC-USD) for ~$289.5M and another ~500 BTC in April. This has reduced its total holdings by ~18% YoY to ~15.68K BTC. 

This means the company booked profits but now has less buffer if prices fall. At the same time, uncertainty around the FOMC meeting and Bitcoin (BTC-USD) near ~$76K is keeping pressure on the stock.

Moreover, RIOT Platforms (RIOT) also amended its $200M loan with Coinbase, shifting to a fixed interest rate and extending maturity by 364 days for better cost clarity, reported SEC filing.

The loan is backed by Bitcoin (BTC-USD), USDC, and cash and needs extra collateral if risk crosses 70%, with possible liquidation at 80%. Lower BTC holdings increase risk if prices fall.

However, ratings are mixed. The quant rating is 2.62 (Hold), while Seeking Alpha analysts rate it 4 (Buy), and Wall Street analysts are more bullish with a 4.57 rating (Strong Buy).

Investors should note that weak estimates, falling BTC holdings, and macro pressure weigh on RIOT platforms (RIOT) stock as it dipped 5% today ahead of Q1 earnings, but long-term sentiment remains positive.

data.title: Meta Platforms options traders price ~7% earnings swing
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) options traders are pricing in a significant post-earnings swing Wednesday, with the company’s May 1 weekly options chain implying a move of roughly $49 per share by Friday’s expiration.

With shares trading around $671 a share in late-morning action, the near-the-money 670 straddle shows traders expecting about 7.3% volatility around the earnings release. Meta (META) is set to report first-quarter results after the market close, with its earnings call set for 5:30 p.m. ET.

Positioning appears especially heavy on the upside. The 700 call carried 10,953 contracts of open interest, while the 710 call held 12,564 contracts, the largest visible concentration in the chain near the post-earnings upside zone. The 750 call also showed 4,346 contracts of open interest.

The put side was active but more dispersed, with the 650 put carrying 4,256 contracts of open interest and the 600 put holding 4,430 contracts, indicating meaningful downside protection well below the stock’s trading level.

With the 670 straddle priced near $49, Meta (META) needs to move beyond roughly $621 or $719 by Friday for buyers of near-the-money volatility to profit. Anything inside that range would leave options traders unrewarded despite a notable move.

Investors will be laser-focused on the company's capital expenditure outlook, particularly in AI, with the company planning $60B-$65B in infrastructure investments this year. Meta (META) closed out last year beating Wall Street expectations for revenues and profitability and setting strong expectations for first-quarter revenues but raised the bar on expected infrastructure spending.

data.title: Wall Street inches lower ahead of Fed decision and Big Tech earnings
data.description:
Wall Street trades lower on Wednesday as traders look ahead to big tech earnings and the Federal Reserve’s latest rate decision.

The blue chip Dow (DJI) was -0.5%, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.1%, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.1%. 

From a sector-by-sector stance, six of the 11 S&P segments were in the red, with health care at the bottom of the list. At the other end of the spectrum, energy was on top as oil (CL1:COM)  pushed back above $100 per barrel. 

U.S. Treasury yields topped 1-month highs. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.39%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) ticked up 5 basis points to 3.89%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) added 4 basis points to 4.98%. 

The Federal Open Market Committee will decide on monetary policy, with rates expected to remain unchanged. The central bank decision is being made as Fed Chair Jerome Powell helms his last meeting, with Fed Chair nominee Kevin Warsh waiting in the wings. 

Elsewhere on the economic calendar, durable goods rebounded more than expected in March. Housing starts rose in March, while building permits fell. The U.S. international trade in goods deficit widened in March, and the wholesale and retail inventories accelerated M/M in March.

Big tech earnings data will continue to be in focus as Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT), and Amazon (AMZN) will release their results after the bell. 

“Stocks are again trading at record highs, reflecting strong investor confidence, but the S&P 500's heavy concentration in the Mag 7 technology leaders elevates downside risk should earnings fall short, as valuations leave little margin for error. For now, current levels are supported by solid forward growth expectations, but that justification could quickly erode if margin compression emerges, inflation weighs on growth, or earnings signal a broader slowdown,” Chris Brigati, chief investment officer of SWBC stated.
